name: Braswell, Margaret
description: A daughter of the gods, Divinely tall and fair, divinely fair.
text: A daughter of the gods, Divinely tall and fair, divinely fair.

name: Cantrall, G. A.
description: His mind his kingdom and his will his law.
text: His mind his kingdom and his will his law.

name: Casey, John E.
clubs: President of Y. M. C. A. '16-'17
description: Formed on the good old plan, A true and brave and downright honest man.
text: Formed on the good old plan, A true and brave and downright honest man.

name: Cheery, Rufus L.
description: A young man with a bright future. Always has a smile and stands well with the ladies.
text: A young man with a bright future. Always has a smile and stands well with the ladies.

name: Clark, J. A.
motto: "The heights by great men reached and kept were not attained by sudden flight, But they while their companions slept were toiling upward in the night."
text: "The heights by great men reached and kept were not attained by sudden flight, But they while their companions slept were toiling upward in the night."

name: Douthit, Jesse C.
clubs: Secretary-Treasurer of A. I. E. E. '17-'18
description: The mildest manners with the bravest mind. A ladies' man. Has many dates.
text: The mildest manners with the bravest mind. A ladies' man. Has many dates.

name: Dyer, J. M.
description: A sturdy son of the good old soil who can use his brain as well as his hands.
text: A sturdy son of the good old soil who can use his brain as well as his hands.

name: Evans, A. O.
description: It must be done like lightning. He is in love.
text: It must be done like lightning. He is in love.

motto: "He did with cheerful will what others talked of while their hands were still."
text: "He did with cheerful will what others talked of while their hands were still."

name: Mccoy, Aileen
description: They call upon her when they want it well done.
text: They call upon her when they want it well done.

name: O'Neal, E. P.
motto: "For he by geometric scale Could take the size of pot and pail, And wisely tell what hour of the day The clock does strike by algebra."
text: "For he by geometric scale Could take the size of pot and pail, And wisely tell what hour of the day The clock does strike by algebra."

name: Rankin, F. S.
motto: "A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance."

text: "A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance."

name: Robison, H. H.
motto: "I am always in haste, but never in a hurry." Why? Because he is an electrical engineer.

text: "I am always in haste, but never in a hurry." Why? Because he is an electrical engineer.

name: Alewine, Boyce
description: He took good care of Jimmie's furniture; such good care that Jimmie himself was unable to find it.
text: He took good care of Jimmie's furniture; such good care that Jimmie himself was unable to find it.

name: Alter, G. K.
motto: "I did not make my A, but I gave the fellow who beat me a good race."
text: "I did not make my A, but I gave the fellow who beat me a good race."

name: Atkinson, Pearl
motto: "Common sense in an uncommon degree is what the world calls wisdom."
text: "Common sense in an uncommon degree is what the world calls wisdom."

motto: "Because I know for me my work is best."
text: "Because I know for me my work is best."

name: Bradley, J. E.
description: Jim has let his Hill Hall environment lead him the wrong way.
text: Jim has let his Hill Hall environment lead him the wrong way.

name: Cantrall, W. M.
description: The very life of the dormitory board of censorship.
text: The very life of the dormitory board of censorship.

description: He will hit the Germans just as hard as he hit his opponent in a football game and that is more than any German can stand.
text: He will hit the Germans just as hard as he hit his opponent in a football game and that is more than any German can stand.

name: Cole, Nell
motto: "Light hearts live long."
text: "Light hearts live long."

motto: "Think I'll join the Navy and keep up the reputation of the Admiral."
text: "Think I'll join the Navy and keep up the reputation of the Admiral."

text: "Boys, all I like about this dormitory council business is that I can do as I please and get by with it."

motto: "All the genius I have is the fruit of labor and thought."
text: "All the genius I have is the fruit of labor and thought."

text: See sophomore writeup.

motto: "What's worth doing at all is worth doing well."
text: "What's worth doing at all is worth doing well."

motto: "For I am but a simple, guileless girl."
text: "For I am but a simple, guileless girl."

motto: "I'm just in love with all the boys."
text: "I'm just in love with all the boys."

text: "No one but she and Heaven knows of what she's thinking."

text: Stars in "Ec 12" because of his knowledge of horticulture.

motto: "Just the airiest, fairest slip of a girl."
text: "Just the airiest, fairest slip of a girl."

text: She cooks the things that Mother used to make.

motto: "He's in the army now."
text: "He's in the army now."

text: A detective of note. Has been accused of being a Senior.

motto: "Sis Cow, she lay low and say nothin'."
text: "Sis Cow, she lay low and say nothin'."

text: A demon with the ladies and a man of note in the military world.

motto: "You just think you have a date with Bess."
text: "You just think you have a date with Bess."

name: Machen, Hughes
description: The class politician who usually gets what he wants.
text: The class politician who usually gets what he wants.

description: George paid the penalty of his crime by serving as a private on the Mexican border.
text: George paid the penalty of his crime by serving as a private on the Mexican border.

motto: "The blue of her eyes beats the blue of the skies."
text: "The blue of her eyes beats the blue of the skies."

description: We wonder how he can remain such a staunch bachelor and still room with Hooker.
text: We wonder how he can remain such a staunch bachelor and still room with Hooker.

description: Looks good in his striped uniform. He made a fortune on the world series.
text: Looks good in his striped uniform. He made a fortune on the world series.

name: McCullough, Gladys
motto: "Nothing ever, hardly ever, troubles me."
text: "Nothing ever, hardly ever, troubles me."

motto: "When study interferes with a good time, cut out the study."
text: "When study interferes with a good time, cut out the study."

motto: "A type of noble womanhood."
text: "A type of noble womanhood."

text: Champion rat catcher of Gray Hall.

text: The medical world will be proud of him.

description: Great on account of his ability to drag himself out of any dilemma into which he may have dragged himself. Ask Prof. Murphy.
text: Great on account of his ability to drag himself out of any dilemma into which he may have dragged himself. Ask Prof. Murphy.

motto: "The way to be loyal to Uncle Sam is to fall in love with a soldier man."
text: "The way to be loyal to Uncle Sam is to fall in love with a soldier man."

description: Has had only one date in three years. Matches pennies for pay.
text: Has had only one date in three years. Matches pennies for pay.

description: He played with a broken arm, but he showed his opponent that his strength all went to his other arm.
text: He played with a broken arm, but he showed his opponent that his strength all went to his other arm.

name: York, H. A.
description: He made the Agri day what it was, and that is saying a good deal.
text: He made the Agri day what it was, and that is saying a good deal.

name: Bradley, M. M.
description: A bold, bad man from Oklahoma.
text: A bold, bad man from Oklahoma.

text: The best freshman among the sophomores.

text: The merry laugh that wins friends.

description: A willing worker and a fine fellow.
text: A willing worker and a fine fellow.

description: Erstwhile schoolma'am.
text: Erstwhile schoolma'am.

text: "Nothing is impossible to a willing mind."

description: Frankly independent, but lovable.
text: Frankly independent, but lovable.

description: The gym star.
text: The gym star.

description: She greatly despises a camouflage.
text: She greatly despises a camouflage.

motto: "I want no uproar, I'm a quiet girl."
text: "I want no uproar, I'm a quiet girl."

text: Never hurries, never worries.

description: She does not live in China, but she surely likes her "Rice".
text: She does not live in China, but she surely likes her "Rice".

text: A tennis expert—especially in love games.

text: She may regret her silence, but never her speech.

text: "Now some girls do giggle and others do chatter, But I give my time to more serious matter."

text: A reformed bachelor and a hard working student.

text: He can't help it because his eyes are mellow brown.

text: "Care is an enemy to life."

text: Has made a close study of women's hearts.

text: "Kenneth, is it Polly tonight?"

text: Nothing could cause her to hurry.

text: The author of Frisco Shine, and an otherwise intelligent young man.

"I am content, I do not care,
Wag as it will, the world for me."

"Sweetness long drawn out."
